userStories.title: Project ID Hard Enforcement
userStories.description: Implement strict project namespace validation on the existing RAG read/write boundary so all guarded service methods reject invalid `projectId` values before any data access. Preserve the current `slug -> projectId` controller flow, treat raw `projectId` validation at the controller boundary as a separate HTTP 400 concern, and keep caller-org ownership as an explicit requirement once auth and project-ownership context exist.
acceptanceCriteria: When `RagService.getOrCreateTable('')` is called, it throws `ForbiddenException` with message `Project ID is required`.
When `RagService.getOrCreateTable('not-a-project-id')` is called, it throws `ForbiddenException`.
When `RagService.getOrCreateTable('cm_invalid_but_well_shaped')` is called and the project does not exist, it throws `ForbiddenException`.
When `RagService.search()` is called with an invalid `projectId`, it rejects before any table access occurs.
Every service method that accepts `projectId` has a doc comment `@throws ForbiddenException if projectId is invalid`.

Current slug-routed API endpoints resolve `slug -> projectId` before calling guarded services; any new endpoint that accepts raw `projectId` as a path/query param returns 400 when the value is missing or malformed.
suggestedCriteria: When `RagService.getOrCreateTable()` rejects an invalid `projectId`, it does not create or update any table-cache entry.
When `RagService.search()` rejects an invalid `projectId`, it does not call LanceDB connection logic or embedding generation.
When a project has been soft-deleted, guarded service methods treat its `projectId` as invalid.
When a malformed `projectId` and a well-formed but non-existent `projectId` are rejected at the service boundary, they produce the same generic forbidden response shape.
When request auth context and project ownership metadata are available, guarded service methods reject a `projectId` that exists but does not belong to the caller org.

userStories.title: KodaDomainWriter Write Gate
userStories.description: Introduce `KodaDomainWriter` as the explicit write gateway for agent-initiated operations. The service should persist canonical ticket/agent events, delegate derived indexing/import work to `RagService`, return provenance-rich `WriteResult` values, and be wired into the existing agent write flow (`AgentsService` in the current codebase) so repositories are no longer called directly from agent write paths.
acceptanceCriteria: When `KodaDomainWriter.writeTicketEvent(data)` is called, it writes a record to `ticket_events` and returns `WriteResult` with `canonicalId`.
When `KodaDomainWriter.writeAgentAction(data)` is called, it writes a record to `agent_events` and returns `WriteResult` with `canonicalId`.
When `KodaDomainWriter.indexDocument(data)` is called, it calls `RagService.indexDocument()` and returns `WriteResult` with `derivedIds`.
When `KodaDomainWriter.importGraphify(data)` is called, it calls `RagService.importGraphify()` and returns `WriteResult`.
Every `WriteResult` includes a fully populated `provenance` field with `actorId`, `projectId`, `action`, `timestamp`, and `source`.
When `writeTicketEvent` is called with a non-existent `projectId`, it throws `ForbiddenException`.
When `RagService.indexDocument()` fails during `indexDocument()`, `WriteResult.error` contains the error message but the canonical write is still committed.
When `KodaDomainWriter` is injected into the existing agent service flow, it replaces direct repository calls for write operations.
suggestedCriteria: When `source` is missing or not one of `agent`, `system`, or `manual`, the write is rejected before any canonical or derived write occurs.
When canonical write succeeds but derived indexing fails, `canonicalId` remains present and the canonical record is not rolled back.
When canonical write succeeds but outbox enqueue fails, the canonical record remains committed and the enqueue failure is logged for retry handling.
When `writeTicketEvent()` is called with an empty `actorId`, it rejects before attempting any write.

userStories.title: Provenance Envelope on Search Responses
userStories.description: Extend the KB search response contract so every successful `RagService.search()` response includes response-level provenance and per-result provenance. This should remain additive to the existing response shape while ensuring the returned result set cannot contain cross-project leakage.
acceptanceCriteria: When `RagService.search()` succeeds, `SearchKbResponseDto.provenance` is non-null.
When `SearchKbResponseDto.results` contains repeated `source_type` + `source_id` pairs, `SearchKbResponseDto.provenance.sources` lists each unique pair only once.
When `KbResultDto.provenance.indexedAt` is serialized, it is a valid ISO timestamp.
When a search result is returned, `KbResultDto.provenance.sourceProjectId` equals the request `projectId`.
When searching with `projectId: 'project-a'`, no returned result reports `provenance.sourceProjectId === 'project-b'`.
When `SearchKbResponseDto.provenance.retrievedAt` is emitted, it is within 1 second of the server-side response timestamp.
suggestedCriteria: When `RagService.search()` returns zero results, `SearchKbResponseDto.provenance.sources` is an empty array.
When a result is mapped without explicit index metadata, `provenance.indexMethod` resolves deterministically to one of `manual`, `import`, or `agent`.
When a result's `sourceProjectId` does not match the request `projectId`, that result is excluded from the response.

userStories.title: Outbox Service Skeleton
userStories.description: Add an outbox skeleton for derived-store reliability so canonical writes can enqueue follow-up work without blocking the request path. The service should persist pending work, process it through a small retry state machine, and support the new domain writer integration.
acceptanceCriteria: When `OutboxService.enqueue(event)` is called, it persists an `outbox_events` record with status `pending`.
When `OutboxService.processPending()` runs, it selects all `pending` records and marks each processed record `completed` or `failed`.
When `KodaDomainWriter` completes a canonical write, it calls `OutboxService.enqueue()` fire-and-forget and does not block the canonical result on outbox completion.
When an outbox event fails repeatedly, it is retried up to 3 times before moving to `dead_letter`.
suggestedCriteria: When `processPending()` is invoked concurrently, the same outbox event is not processed twice.
When `processPending()` retries a failed event, `retryCount` increments atomically with the status update.
When an outbox event reaches `dead_letter`, it is no longer selected by subsequent `processPending()` runs.
When an outbox event fails, the last error message is persisted for inspection.
When multiple pending events are processed, they are handled in a stable `createdAt` order.

analysis:
This phase should be executed against the real `apps/api` surface, not against speculative abstractions. The immediate guardrail is in `apps/api/src/rag/rag.service.ts`, with `apps/api/src/rag/rag.controller.ts` already following the intended pattern of resolving `slug -> project.id` before service calls. There is no current `rag.repository.ts`, `docs.service.ts`, or singular `AgentService`; the existing write-path integration point is `apps/api/src/agents/agents.service.ts`, with related mutation behavior also living in ticket transition services. Phase 0 should therefore harden the existing RAG and agent mutation boundaries first, and only introduce new abstractions where the stories explicitly require them.

For project validation, the enforceable boundary today is: non-empty raw input, CUID-shaped internal project identifier, and an existing non-deleted project record in `projects`. Controller-level raw `projectId` inputs should reject missing or malformed values with HTTP 400 before service entry. Service-level guarded methods should reject invalid or non-existent project ids with `ForbiddenException` before any LanceDB table access, cache mutation, embedding generation, or derived-store activity. The source spec also requires caller-org ownership checks. Because the current schema does not yet expose the org or tenant linkage needed to enforce that cleanly, the PRD should keep this as an explicit schema/auth prerequisite or follow-up decision rather than treating existence-only validation as full completion.

The provenance and writer work are valid but need pragmatic boundaries. `SearchKbResponseDto` and `KbResultDto` changes are additive at the payload level, but they still change the OpenAPI contract and require `openapi.json` and generated client updates in lockstep. The `sourceProjectId` check should stay as a defensive invariant even if LanceDB is keyed by project today: a mismatched result must never be returned. For `KodaDomainWriter`, provenance `source` should be passed explicitly in write inputs instead of inferred implicitly. Canonical writes should succeed or fail transactionally, and derived failures must not roll back canonical state. The story-level acceptance criteria still allow `indexDocument()` to surface a derived failure in `WriteResult`, but outbox enqueue failures should at minimum be logged and retried rather than blocking canonical completion. Existing tests will need fixture updates because many current RAG tests use ad-hoc project ids rather than real persisted CUID-backed project records.
